index.title: Meteorological Licenses & Permits Services
index.card1.title: New Service Request
index.card1.desc: Submit a request for a permit or license to practice as a weather observer. You can issue a new permit, renew an existing one, or request a replacement.
index.card2.title: Track My Requests
index.card2.desc: Track the status of your submitted requests, view details, see approvals, and download issued permits or licenses.
index.footer: © 2024 National Center for Meteorology - All rights reserved

success.title: Request Submitted Successfully
success.msg: Your request has been submitted to the relevant department. The attached documents and provided data will be reviewed and you will be contacted as soon as possible.
success.next.title: What happens next?
success.next.text: The review team will evaluate your request and verify the attachments. The process may take 6 to 23 business days. You will receive an email notification when the request status is updated.
terms.text_block:
Welcome to the Weather Observer Permit/License Request Service.

Before proceeding, you must review and agree to the service terms and conditions. These terms include rules and obligations related to practicing the profession and the requirements for obtaining a license or permit.
conditions.html_title: Terms and Conditions - National Center for Meteorology
conditions.header_subtitle: Permit/License to Practice the Profession of Observer
conditions.section1.label: First:
conditions.section1.title: The license applicant must submit the following documents:
conditions.section1.req1: Apply using the forms approved by the Center.
conditions.section1.req2: Proof of payment of the prescribed service fee.
conditions.section1.req3: A valid copy of the national ID or residence permit (Iqama), with the original presented for verification, provided that the applicant is not less than 18 years old.
conditions.section1.req5: A certificate of experience issued by the General Organization for Social Insurance showing previous employment periods (GOSI printout).
conditions.section1.req6_intro: A certificate of experience from a meteorological observation facility/institution accredited by the National Center for Meteorology, confirming that the applicant has completed the following required experience periods:
conditions.section1.req6_sub1: Forecaster: experience of not less than six months.
conditions.section1.req6_sub2: Observer: experience of not less than six months.
conditions.section1.req7_intro: The original, duly attested and accredited academic qualification, provided that the following conditions are met:
conditions.section1.req7_sub1: The qualification must be issued by one of the officially accredited Saudi institutions, or evidence of equivalency issued by the competent national authorities must be provided for qualifications obtained abroad.
conditions.section1.req7_sub2_intro: The qualification obtained by the applicant must cover the aspects required to practice any of the professions referred to at the beginning of this guide, as follows:
conditions.section1.req7_bullet1: • It must include all components of the basic education program required for an applicant seeking a license to practice the profession of Forecaster (Bachelor’s degree in Meteorology).
conditions.section1.req7_bullet2: • It must include all components of the basic education program required for an applicant seeking a license to practice the profession of Observer (Diploma in Meteorology).
conditions.section1.req8: Evidence that the applicant for a permit/license has obtained practical qualification or vocational training in one of the meteorological fields from an accredited regional center or a competent national authority. This requirement is waived for those who have previously worked as a meteorological specialist or technician at any official national center or authority, provided that an official certificate proving this is submitted.
conditions.section1.req9: A recent certificate of no criminal record addressed to the National Center for Meteorology (original).
conditions.section1.req11: A declaration undertaking the accuracy of all submitted documents and assuming full criminal and civil liability in the event that any of them are found to be inaccurate.
conditions.section1.req12: The Center may add any other requirements or conditions as it deems appropriate.
conditions.section2.label: Second:
conditions.section2.title: An applicant for (license renewal) must submit the following documents:
conditions.section2.req2: Payment of the prescribed service fee.
conditions.section2.req4: Submission of the original previous license.
conditions.section2.req5_intro: A license holder nominated to obtain a higher category in the professional specialization, in accordance with the registration grading system, must submit the following documents:
conditions.section2.req5_sub1: Certificate from General Organization for Social Insurance.
conditions.section2.req5_sub2: An experience certificate approved by the meteorological entity to which the license holder belongs.
conditions.section2.req5_sub3: A declaration confirming the accuracy of the documents.
conditions.section3.label: Third:
conditions.section3.title: An applicant for (issuance of a replacement for a lost license) must submit the following documents:
conditions.section3.req3: Evidence of submitting a report to the Center regarding the loss of the professional practice card.
conditions.section4.title: General Provisions
conditions.section4.req1: The originals of all documents must be attached for review.
conditions.section4.req2: An applicant applying for a license for the first time or for renewal must not have had their license previously revoked by a decision of the Center for behavioral or technical reasons, unless they have rectified their status and provided reasons acceptable to the Center.
conditions.section4.req3: A license applicant may not engage in any meteorological work or activities until obtaining the required permit or license. The same prohibition applies to anyone whose license has expired and has not been renewed within the prescribed time.
conditions.section4.req4: If the required experience periods are not available for a first-time license applicant, the Center may issue a temporary permit valid for a period of six (6) months, for one time only, upon the beneficiary’s request, to enable them to complete the required experience periods.
conditions.section4.req5: A license applicant may authorize another person to complete or follow up any procedure related to issuing, renewing, or replacing a lost license.
conditions.section4.req6: A license holder may practice meteorological professions only through a meteorological observation facility licensed and accredited by the National Center for Meteorology.
conditions.section4.req7_intro: The license or renewal applicant must not be a government employee, except for the following cases:
conditions.section4.req7_sub1: • Staff members of the National Center for Meteorology who practice meteorological professions.
conditions.section4.req7_sub2: • Those who are on officially approved leave from their employer, upon submitting official proof thereof.
conditions.section4.req8: The license applicant must be of good conduct and behavior and must not have been convicted by a final court judgment or dismissed from any position for reasons involving dishonor or breach of trust, unless their civil rights have been restored.
conditions.section4.req9: The license/renewal applicant must comply with the requirements and timelines specified by the service provider (the Center) to complete the procedures for the requested service.
conditions.section4.req10: The license holder (renewal applicant) must submit the renewal application within a period not exceeding thirty (30) days prior to the license expiry date.
conditions.section4.req11: The rules and provisions governing the issuance of meteorological licenses and permits are subject to the Meteorology Law, its Executive Regulations, and any guides, decisions, or circulars issued by the National Center for Meteorology in this regard.
